name: Eskom Holdings SOC Limited
description: Eskom is South Africa's state-owned electricity utility and one of the largest power producers in the world. It generates, transmits and distributes electricity to industrial, mining, commercial, agricultural and residential customers. Eskom faces significant challenges including load-shedding, aging infrastructure and a debt burden exceeding R400 billion.

name: Transnet SOC Limited
description: Transnet is South Africa's state-owned freight transport and logistics company. It operates the national rail network, ports and pipelines. Transnet's operations are critical to the South African economy, handling the bulk of freight movement across the country.

name: South African Airways SOC Limited
description: South African Airways is the national carrier of South Africa. Following business rescue proceedings in 2020, SAA was restructured and relaunched in 2021. The Takatso Consortium acquired a 51% stake in the airline as part of the privatisation process, with the South African government retaining 49%.

name: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
description: PRASA is responsible for providing rail commuter services in South Africa's metropolitan areas and long-distance passenger rail services. PRASA faces significant infrastructure challenges including aging rolling stock, vandalism and cable theft, which have severely impacted service delivery.

name: Denel SOC Limited
description: Denel is South Africa's state-owned defence and technology company. It designs, develops and manufactures a wide range of defence products including missiles, artillery, aircraft and armoured vehicles. Denel has faced severe financial difficulties including inability to pay salaries and loss of key contracts.

name: South African Broadcasting Corporation SOC Limited
description: The SABC is South Africa's public broadcaster, operating television and radio services in all 11 official languages. The SABC has faced persistent financial challenges, governance failures and political interference. It provides a critical public service mandate including news, education and entertainment.

name: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
description: ACSA manages and operates nine South African airports including O.R. Tambo International Airport (Johannesburg), Cape Town International Airport and King Shaka International Airport (Durban). ACSA is a key enabler of South Africa's aviation sector and tourism industry.
